S002.notes: The operations structure documentation is described as delivered but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The menus include allergen-relevant items (smoked eel, Baltic herring, porcini, lamb, duck, pike perch, dairy, rye) and the à la carte and tasting menus are bilingual but no allergen labelling or EU FIC 1169/2011 compliance framework is mentioned anywhere in the documentation. At pre-opening stage, no allergen management system is confirmed in place.

S005.notes: Operations documentation including 'minimum SOPs and daily routine structure' was delivered in early March but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' A cleaning schedule would be part of this documentation but its existence and adoption is unconfirmed. No specific mention of a cleaning schedule in the delivered materials.

S007.notes: The operations documentation covers onboarding and SOPs but no specific mention of food hygiene certification requirements for the team. Chef candidates (Vidas and Andrius) have not yet started. No reference to Level 2 food hygiene certificates or equivalent Lithuanian food safety training requirements being tracked or required.

S008.notes: The operations structure documentation is described as covering 'minimum SOPs and daily routine structure' but no HACCP documentation is specifically mentioned. Given that the owners have not confirmed reviewing the operations documentation, and no HACCP system is referenced in the full description of deliverables, this is a significant pre-opening gap.

S018.notes: No internal food safety audit process is mentioned in the operations documentation deliverables. Given the pre-opening stage and the fact that the operations documentation has not been confirmed as reviewed, no self-assessment or audit cadence has been established.

S045.notes: Recipe cards and portion standards are not mentioned in the operations documentation deliverables. The menus are developed but no reference to standardised recipe cards or portion control documentation being created alongside them. The operations documentation covers 'minimum SOPs' but has not been confirmed as reviewed by owners.

S051.notes: Menus have been developed as HTML files and delivered, but no mention of written recipe cards or drink specification standards being created for the team. The operations documentation covers SOPs but recipe standards are not listed among the deliverables. Chef candidates have not yet started, so no kitchen standards have been established.

S054.notes: An onboarding process is described as part of the operations documentation delivered in early March, but 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The onboarding programme exists on paper but has not been validated, adopted, or tested. Chef candidates visit March 24 — no confirmed onboarding readiness.

S056.notes: No team meetings or briefing structure is described as being in place. The operations documentation covers 'daily routine structure' but this has not been confirmed as reviewed. No team exists yet to meet, but no briefing cadence has been established for when they do.

S058.notes: The consultant (Matas Paulinas) is the single point of knowledge, coordination, and execution across brand, operations, vendor management, hiring, menus, website, and financial setup. 'You had to contact him [Vidas] yourself because the owners weren't sharing information.' 'You called him [Andrius] directly.' The entire pre-opening project is dependent on one external consultant with no internal operational counterpart confirmed. If Matas exits before handover, the project collapses.

S061.notes: The owners (Simona and Rolandas) are not functioning as operational decision-makers. The consultant is executing everything: 'brand coordination, operations structuring, vendor management, staff hiring strategy, physical branding, menu development, onboarding documentation, financial setup coordination — essentially everything.' There is no evidence of the owners taking protected management or strategic time — rather, they are absent from the process entirely. Severity override: this is more severe than the signal's default — the owners are not working every shift, they are not working at all on the project.

S065.notes: No reference to food safety certification requirements being tracked for incoming staff. Chef candidates (Vidas and Andrius) have not yet started. The operations documentation does not specifically mention food safety certification as a hiring requirement. This is a pre-opening gap.

S069.notes: The operations documentation includes 'daily routine structure' and 'minimum SOPs' but these have not been confirmed as reviewed by the owners. No opening or closing checklist is specifically mentioned among the deliverables. Pre-opening gap.

S096.notes: Menus have been developed with pricing (à la carte mains €24-28, tasting menu €105, wine pairing €65, glass range €7-12, bottles €34-95) but no mention of individual menu item costings or gross margin calculations being performed. The HR cost dynamics tool was built for salary ranges but no food or beverage cost modelling is mentioned among the deliverables.

S102.notes: No weekly or monthly financial review rhythm is mentioned. The HR cost dynamics tool was delivered but the DSS and accounting contract with Audronė at Verslo Struktūra 'has been floating since February 26 and it's unclear if it's signed.' No financial reporting structure is confirmed in place.

S105.notes: No cash flow forecasting or forward financial planning is mentioned. The DSS contract is unsigned, the accounting relationship is unconfirmed, and no budget or forecast document is referenced among the deliverables. The salary ranges question 'has been open since January.'

S106.notes: No annual budget or financial forecast is mentioned among the deliverables. The HR cost dynamics tool addresses salary ranges but no revenue forecast, operating budget, or pre-opening cost plan is referenced. The DSS contract is unconfirmed.

S116.notes: No social media strategy is mentioned in the deliverables. The FOH manager posting strategy references 'personal profile post first, then HR groups, then Stories, then Kalvos accounts' but this is a hiring tactic, not a content strategy. The Kalvos social media accounts are referenced but no content plan, posting cadence, or platform strategy is described.

S117.notes: No mention of a Google Business Profile being set up, claimed, or optimised. The website is built and the visit section includes location and hours (Wednesday through Sunday) but no Google Business Profile setup is referenced in the deliverables.

S120.notes: Instagram is referenced only in the context of the FOH hiring post ('then Stories, then Kalvos accounts'). No Instagram content strategy, photography plan, or pre-launch content calendar is mentioned. The website is built but photography is still pending ('waiting for final photography').

S124.notes: The website is 'waiting for final photography.' The menus are developed but physical menu design by Nomeda is waiting on approvals. No photography has been completed. 'All coded, all branded, all waiting for final photography and confirmed operational details.' This is a confirmed gap blocking both the website launch and physical menu production.

S125.notes: No email list, newsletter, or direct customer communication channel is mentioned in the deliverables. The website has a 'reserve' section but no CRM or email capture mechanism is referenced.

S126.notes: No customer acquisition strategy beyond the website and social media presence is mentioned. The venue is in a small town in Žemaitija — a destination concept that requires active outreach to drive awareness. No PR strategy, press outreach, influencer engagement, or launch campaign is referenced in the deliverables.

S127.notes: No outreach to food bloggers, press, or influencers is mentioned. For a destination restaurant in a small Lithuanian town, press and PR are critical to launch success. No launch PR plan is referenced among the deliverables.

S128.notes: No marketing calendar or launch campaign plan is mentioned. The website, menus, and brand are ready but no planned promotional activity for the opening period is referenced. The seasonal menu concept implies seasonal marketing would be relevant but no calendar exists.

S145.notes: No reporting dashboards or performance analytics are mentioned in the deliverables. The HR cost dynamics tool is the only financial tool referenced. No POS reporting, revenue tracking, or operational analytics framework is described.

S149.notes: No CRM or customer data capture mechanism is mentioned. The website has a reserve section but no email capture, booking system integration, or guest preference tracking is referenced.

S152.notes: The website is built but 'waiting for final photography and confirmed operational details.' No booking system, social media integration, or Google Business Profile is mentioned as being connected to the website. The online presence components exist in isolation.

S154.notes: No written business plan is mentioned among the deliverables. The consultant has built operational documentation, HR tools, and financial tools, but no strategic business plan document is referenced. The owners' vision is articulated conceptually but not in a formal business plan.

S166.notes: No team is in place yet, but the operations documentation covering standards and expected behaviours 'has not been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' The owners cannot align a team on standards they have not themselves reviewed or adopted.

S167.notes: The operations documentation includes job descriptions, responsibility and reporting logic, onboarding process, work discipline and communication model, minimum SOPs, and daily routine structure — but this is described as 'none of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.' A staff handbook may be embedded in this documentation but its existence as a standalone reference document is not confirmed.

S169.notes: The owner vision is articulated by the consultant ('slowness, nature, family, and grounding,' 'a place to arrive, not just to eat') but the owners themselves are not engaging with the operational documentation, not responding to emails, not attending agreed meetings, and have not reviewed the menus, SOPs, or hiring materials. 'Rolandas still hasn't looked at them [the menus] as of mid-March.' The vision exists in the consultant's documents but has not been shared with or validated by the team — because there is no team yet, and the owners are not present in the process.

S187.notes: The entire pre-opening project knowledge is held by the consultant (Matas Paulinas). The owners are not engaged with the deliverables. If the consultant exits, there is no documented handover protocol and the owners have not reviewed or internalised the brand, operations, hiring, or financial documentation. 'Nine deliverables are complete but many are waiting for someone to look at them.' This is an acute key-person dependency risk at the most critical project stage.

S190.notes: No health and safety risk assessment is mentioned in the deliverables. The operations documentation covers SOPs and daily routines but no H&S risk assessment is referenced. The consultant is physically working on site ('you're physically at Kalvos, working on staff facilities') with no confirmed H&S documentation.

S191.notes: No fire risk assessment or fire safety compliance is mentioned in the deliverables. The 'technical inspection checklist' is listed as waiting for owner response but its content is not described. Pre-opening fire safety compliance is a legal requirement before opening.

S193.notes: The bar menu includes alcohol (cocktails, craft beers, spirits, wine). No mention of an alcohol licence being obtained or in process. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. Licensing compliance is a pre-opening requirement.

S194.notes: Employment contracts are not mentioned in the deliverables. The operations documentation covers job descriptions and responsibility logic but no employment contract templates or compliance with Lithuanian labour law is referenced. Chef candidates have not yet started.

S195.notes: No gas safety certificate or electrical installation condition report is mentioned. The property is a converted building with a restaurant, kitchen, and 9 hotel rooms. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. These are pre-opening legal requirements.

S211.notes: The DSS contract with Audronė at Verslo Struktūra 'has been floating since February 26 and it's unclear if it's signed.' DSS in Lithuania handles VAT and tax reporting. Without a confirmed accounting and tax compliance partner, i.SAF VAT declaration compliance is at risk. This is a pre-opening legal requirement.

S212.notes: No POS system is mentioned in the deliverables. Without a POS system integrated with Lithuanian tax authority electronic reporting requirements, the venue cannot legally operate. This is a pre-opening gap.

S213.notes: The DSS contract is unconfirmed. No systematic process for tracking mandatory social insurance contributions or filing employer notifications for new hires is mentioned. Chef candidates are about to be hired with no confirmed HR compliance infrastructure.

S214.notes: No food business operator approval certificate is mentioned in the deliverables. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. This is a pre-opening legal requirement in Lithuania.

S215.notes: The bar menu includes alcohol (cocktails, craft beers on tap, spirits, wine). No alcohol licence is mentioned in the deliverables. The 'technical inspection checklist' is waiting for owner response. An alcohol licence is a pre-opening legal requirement.

S216.notes: Employment contracts are not mentioned in the deliverables. The salary ranges question 'has been open since January.' Chef candidates are about to be hired with no confirmed employment contract framework. Lithuanian labour law compliance is not referenced.

S218.notes: The property has a garden and outdoor spaces. 'The garden measurements since February' are listed as a blocked open item. No outdoor terrace strategy, permits, or seasonal setup plan is mentioned. The garden measurements being unresolved since February suggests the outdoor space planning is stalled.

S219.notes: The venue is positioned as a destination in Žemaičių Kalvarija — a pilgrimage town that attracts religious tourists, particularly in summer. No segmentation strategy between pilgrimage/religious tourists, destination diners, and local regulars is mentioned. The concept is built for a specific audience but no differentiated service or pricing approach for different visitor types is described.

raw_input_text:
Okay, here's everything I know about Kalvos from the beginning to today — the full picture.

---

Kalvos is a destination restaurant and boutique stay in Žemaičių Kalvarija, a small town in the Plungė district of Žemaitija. It's owned by Simona Gimbutienė and Rolandas Gimbutas. The concept is built around slowness, nature, family, and grounding. It's not a local café, not a generic countryside getaway — it's positioned as a real destination with emotional depth. The tagline energy is "a place to arrive, not just to eat."

The property has a restaurant, 9 boutique rooms, private dining with two large round tables, a garden, a greenhouse, and event spaces. The cuisine direction is Nordic-Baltic fusion — seasonal, local, ingredient-first, modern Lithuanian with depth. The address is Vienuolyno g. 2, Žemaičių Kalvarija.

You, Matas Paulinas, are the hospitality consultant running the entire pre-launch project through your company MPCM — Matas Paulinas Consulting & Management. Your role is comprehensive: brand coordination, operations structuring, vendor management, staff hiring strategy, physical branding, menu development, onboarding documentation, financial setup coordination — essentially everything that needs to happen between "we have a building" and "we are open."

---

**The brand.**

The brandbook was created with Nomeda from Brand Me. It's solid — a full design system with specific colors (dark green #2d3526, cream #eee9e3, olive #5a6b4f), typography (Dashiell Fine for headings, Figtree for body, Emily Austin for accent), the hills symbol as the core brand mark, and a full material language: blind deboss, letterpress, natural uncoated stock, recycled leather, linen, cork, brass, corten steel. The brand is tactile and quiet — nature should frame everything, not a billboard.

You built a comprehensive physical branding touchpoints guide covering 8 zones: restaurant table (menu, napkins, ceramics, coasters, candle holders, bill presenter, wine list), bar and drinks (cocktail napkins, ice stamp, custom labels, stirrers), arrival and reception (exterior signage, door detail, welcome card, business cards, wax seal, stationery), hotel room (key card, bed linen, bathrobes, amenities, soap, slippers, room directory, coffee set, water carafe, laundry bag), bathroom (hand towels, soap dispenser, amenity bottles, candle, mirror detail), staff and kitchen (aprons, waiter aprons, order pads, name pins, prep labels, kitchen towels), takeaway and gifts (preserves, gift vouchers, tote bag, gift box, postcards, matchboxes), and outdoor (garden signage, wayfinding, herb markers, outdoor blankets, event signage).

---

**The website.**

You built the full Kalvos website — structure and branded HTML/CSS front-end. Eight sections: home, our story, restaurant, stay, events, experience, visit, and reserve. It's ready for content population and deployment. The hero says "A place to arrive, not just to eat." The story section covers Simona and Rolandas, why Žemaičių Kalvarija, the philosophy. Restaurant shows seasonal menu preview and the breakfast/lunch/dinner/tasting structure. Stay highlights the 9 rooms. Events covers private dining, celebrations, workshops, retreats, garden events. There's a visit section with location, hours (Wednesday through Sunday), parking, FAQ. All coded, all branded, all waiting for final photography and confirmed operational details.

---

**The menus.**

You developed the full menu suite in early March. Four separate menus, all delivered as editable HTML files:

The bar menu — 8 signature cocktails with a botanical and foraged direction: Birch & Smoke (birch sap, mezcal, charred rosemary), The Greenhouse (gin, cucumber, elderflower), Forest Floor (rye whiskey, chanterelle syrup), Sea Buckthorn Spritz, Amber Negroni (Lithuanian gin, pine tincture), Meadow Sour (bourbon, meadowsweet), Kalvos Mule (house ginger-rhubarb), After the Rain (pear brandy, thyme). Six bar snacks with pairing recommendations. Three Lithuanian craft beers on tap — Dundulis pilsner, Sakiškių wheat ale, Genys dark lager. Bottled beer and cider, spirits, three non-alcoholic cocktails (Garden Tonic, Birch Fizz, Berry & Thyme Shrub), soft drinks including house lemonade and seasonal kombucha.

The tasting menu — €105, with wine pairing at €65 and non-alcoholic at €35. Ten courses: two snacks (pine salt cracker with smoked curd; beetroot chip with horseradish and roe), two cold courses (cured Baltic herring; smoked eel), five warm courses (spring onion with whey butter; porcini broth; pike perch; duck breast with beetroot and black currant; lamb shoulder with wild garlic), and sweet (birch sap granita, honey and rye dessert, petit fours). Each course has a paired wine — all natural, small-producer: Enderle & Moll, Gut Oggau, Sybille Kuntz, Domaine Labet, Claus Preisinger, Milan Nestarec, Domaine de la Borde, Dorogi Tokaji.

The à la carte — 5 snacks, 3 cold appetisers (beetroot tartare, smoked trout, cured Baltic herring), 3 warm appetisers (seasonal soup, porcini broth, spring onion), 3 mains in the €24-28 range (pike perch, slow-cooked pork shoulder, duck breast), 3 desserts (honey and rye, dark chocolate mousse, apple and quark), sweet bites for coffee.

The wine card — two pages. Page one: sparkling (Crémant d'Alsace, Prosecco Col Fondo, Champagne Laherte Frères), whites (Müller-Thurgau, Grüner Veltliner Gut Oggau, Riesling Sybille Kuntz, Savagnin Domaine Labet, Chenin Blanc Domaine Mosse, Chablis Pattes Loup), rosé (Blaufränkisch Claus Preisinger), orange and skin-contact (Ribolla Gialla Radikon, Furmint Dobogó). Page two: reds (Pinot Noir Nestarec, Trousseau Domaine de la Borde, Zweigelt Judith Beck, Gamay Marcel Lapierre Morgon, Nerello Mascalese Frank Cornelissen Susucaru, Côtes du Rhône Gramenon La Sagesse, Barolo Giovanni Rosso at €95), sweet wines (Tokaji Dorogi, Muscat de Rivesaltes Cazes), digestifs (grappa Marolo, calvados Dupont, Amaro Montenegro, Fernet-Branca, house Krupnikas, cognac Pierre Ferrand, pear eau de vie), coffee and tea. Glass range €7-12, bottles €34-95. Two price columns: glass and bottle.

Simona liked the menus. Rolandas still hasn't looked at them as of mid-March. The menus are now going to be bilingual — Lithuanian and English. Nomeda has been briefed and asked to design the physical menus from scratch using the brandbook and your content. Her task: layout, typography, paper selection, print preparation. The restaurant menu is A4 hardcover, leather or linen bound, blind deboss hills symbol, with seasonal inserts. The tasting menu is a single-page insert. The wine card is separate but same material language. The bar menu is lighter format — single card or folded, heavier stock.

---

**The team and operations.**

You built the entire operations structure documentation: job descriptions for kitchen, FOH, and admin positions, responsibility and reporting logic, onboarding process, work discipline and communication model, minimum SOPs, and daily routine structure. You created a FOH manager job posting — a really personal, values-driven text that talks about Kalvos as a home where the administrator is the host. The strategy was personal profile post first, then HR groups, then Stories, then Kalvos accounts. You created an HR cost dynamics tool for Rolandas to set salary ranges. All of this was delivered in early March. None of it has been confirmed as reviewed by the owners.

For the chef position, there are two candidates. Vidas — who was supposed to start but didn't because his current workplace has staff shortages. You had to contact him yourself because the owners weren't sharing information. He's still wrapping up his old job. Then there's Andrius — who nearly got lost because he sent a menu, nobody responded for a week, he assumed they weren't interested and decided to stay at his current restaurant. You called him directly, figured out what happened, told Simona, and she managed to recover the relationship. Both are confirmed to visit Kalvos on March 24. You're there for that.

For FOH manager, Simona posted something and got some responses, but you haven't seen any candidate profiles or details.

---

**The vendors and partners.**

Nomeda at Brand Me handles physical branding and print design. She's ready to go, waiting on your approvals to start. Deimantė does exterior signage — she quoted €2,250 + VAT plus €320 + VAT for installation, including handling municipality permits. Rolandas decided to check an alternative through his BNI business club contact instead. No update on that. Audronė at Verslo Struktūra handles DSS and accounting services — her contract has been floating since February 26 and it's unclear if it's signed. Karolina does interior design — she sent stone samples for the bar (quartz brown at €135 per square meter, a nicer alternative at €200, slab about 5 square meters) and confirmed a deal with furniture maker Kubilius for bar finishing. No response from owners on that. Jokado does tableware — new account manager, proposal being recalculated, you're visiting to choose napkin models.

---

**The communication problem.**

This is the single biggest operational risk on the project, and it's been documented repeatedly. The pattern is: you send emails with detailed deliverables and questions → no reply for one to two weeks → you have to call → you get partial verbal information → you agree to meet → the meeting gets postponed or cancelled → the cycle restarts.

This has been going on since at least January. You've documented it formally in multiple emails. The clearest consequence was nearly losing Andrius — a real chef candidate, gone because nobody replied to his email for a week. That's the preview of what happens with future staff, vendors, and eventually guests.

You sent a major status email on March 3 covering over ten items. Then another on March 5 pushing DSS and signage. Then menus and signage drafts on March 6. You called Simona on March 10, went through everything verbal

coverage_gaps: Financial performance data — no revenue, cost, or margin figures available; venue not yet operational
Food safety operational controls — no evidence of HACCP, allergen management, or temperature monitoring systems being in place or tested
POS and technology infrastructure — no POS system, booking platform, or payment system mentioned
Alcohol and food business licensing status — referenced indirectly through the technical inspection checklist but not confirmed
Opening date — no target launch date is mentioned anywhere in the text
Insurance coverage — no reference to public liability, employer liability, or property insurance
Supplier agreements — no confirmed suppliers for food, coffee, or beverages beyond the wine list producers
Customer acquisition and launch marketing plan — no PR, influencer, or launch campaign strategy described
Photography — explicitly absent and blocking both website launch and physical menu production
FOH manager hiring — Simona posted something and got responses but no candidate details or progress is described
Building and premises compliance — technical inspection checklist is waiting for owner response; fire safety, gas, and electrical certificates not confirmed

unmapped_observations.evidence: The pattern is: you send emails with detailed deliverables and questions → no reply for one to two weeks → you have to call → you get partial verbal information → you agree to meet → the meeting gets postponed or cancelled → the cycle restarts. The salary ranges question has been open since January. The garden measurements since February. The DSS contract since late February. Multiple deliverables are complete but sitting idle because there's no response.
unmapped_observations.observation: Owner decision paralysis as a systemic project risk — not a standard operational signal

unmapped_observations.evidence: Andrius — who nearly got lost because he sent a menu, nobody responded for a week, he assumed they weren't interested and decided to stay at his current restaurant. You called him directly, figured out what happened, told Simona, and she managed to recover the relationship.
unmapped_observations.observation: Candidate relationship management failure — nearly lost a key chef hire due to owner non-responsiveness

unmapped_observations.evidence: You had to contact him [Vidas] yourself because the owners weren't sharing information. Rolandas still hasn't looked at them [the menus] as of mid-March. Nine deliverables are complete but many are waiting for someone to look at them.
unmapped_observations.observation: Consultant-owner information asymmetry — consultant knows more about the project than the owners

unmapped_observations.evidence: Rolandas decided to check an alternative through his BNI business club contact instead. No update on that. Karolina sent stone samples for the bar... No response from owners on that.

unmapped_observations.observation: Accounting and legal compliance infrastructure unconfirmed at pre-opening stage

unmapped_observations.evidence: Where things stand right now — late March 2026. Four things are blocked... Seven things are waiting for owner response... Five things are in progress... Nine deliverables are complete but many are waiting for someone to look at them.
unmapped_observations.observation: No confirmed opening date — the text describes a pre-opening project with no target launch date mentioned anywhere

unmapped_observations.evidence: The tasting menu — €105, with wine pairing at €65 and non-alcoholic at €35. Ten courses... Barolo Giovanni Rosso at €95. Glass range €7-12, bottles €34-95.
